PixiJS V5 Tutorial (17) - Adding Text to Your HP Bar

Last time we built an HP bar, but what about the text on it? In this post we cover the easy part — adding text on top of the bar with PIXI.Text, swapping in a variable for the current HP, and using the game loop (ticker) to drain health and update the number in real time.
JavaScript Core Concepts (9): Threads, Sync, and Async — Why Order Matters

This is where we get into one of the most important ideas in JavaScript — the whole sync vs async, single-threaded thing that trips so many people up. We'll start with what a thread even is, why JavaScript can only do one thing at a time, and how the Event queue lets it handle async work anyway.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (16) - Building a Game HP Bar

Starting from the last post, our project finally started to feel a bit like an actual game. So in this one we'll build something every game needs — an HP bar. We'll draw it ourselves with two stacked rounded rectangles and a controllable width property to represent the health percentage.
JavaScript Core Concepts (8): How Memory Gets Allocated and Freed (Garbage Collection)

Let's talk about how JavaScript reclaims memory. This post covers what garbage collection actually is, why older languages made you manage memory by hand, the rule JavaScript uses to decide when an object is safe to collect, and a hands-on Chrome experiment to watch memory get allocated and freed in real time.
How to Add ESLint to Vue CLI
Most projects add a code style manager (ESLint) to keep code quality consistent — but how should you actually add it to a Vue CLI project?
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (15) - Collision Detection with Bump

Last time we learned how to draw geometric shapes in PIXI. Now it's time for something I find pretty interesting — collision detection. If you've ever Googled "PIXI hitTestRectangle" and hit a wall, this post explains why, and shows you a couple of ready-to-use functions you can drop straight into your project.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (14) - Graphics (2): Circles, Polygons, Lines and Stars

Last time we covered basic geometry drawing in PixiJS, but only the rectangle. PIXI actually gives you a bunch of other shapes to draw too — circles, ellipses, rounded rectangles, polygons, lines and even stars. So this post walks through all the other geometry methods one by one.
JavaScript Core Concepts (7): not defined VS undefined — What's the Difference?

not defined and undefined are two really important concepts in JavaScript, and they trip people up all the time. Once you understand the difference between them, debugging gets a whole lot easier. This post walks through what each one actually means, and why you should never use undefined as a value or a variable name.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (13) - Graphics (1): Drawing Shapes

Time to draw some geometry with PIXI. In this post we'll go through drawing shapes with PixiJS Graphics — outlines, rectangles, fills — and then tie it back to the interaction tricks from earlier chapters so a shape becomes something you can actually click.
JavaScript Core Concepts (6): Hoisting — Why You Can Use a Variable Before You Declare It

Hoisting is honestly one of my favorite JavaScript concepts. Every variable and every function in JavaScript has it. In this post we'll dig into why you can use a variable before it's even declared, why you get undefined instead of an error, and why functions get higher priority than variables. Plus a little quiz at the end to test yourself.
